title: Indulgent Okinawan Turkey Skillet
description: A malty, crumbly, lazy-sunday Japanese (Okinawan) dish featuring turkey, sautéed with anti-inflammatory-friendly ingredients.
instructions: Heat sesame oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering.
Add sliced turkey breast, season with salt and pepper, and sear until browned on all sides, about 4 minutes.
Remove turkey from skillet and set aside.
In the same skillet, sauté minced garlic and grated ginger for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Stir in soy sauce, mirin, miso paste, and brown sugar, mixing until the sauce thickens slightly, about 2 minutes.
Return the turkey to the skillet, spooning the sauce over the meat, and cook until the turkey reaches an internal temperature of 165°F, about 5 minutes.
Scatter sliced scallions and toasted sesame seeds over the skillet and stir to combine.
Let the mixture simmer for another 2 minutes to allow flavors to meld.
Serve hot, optionally over steamed quinoa or cauliflower rice for a low-carb option.
